pi_items.source: verifier.py uses HSV masking for red color
verifier.py uses a hardcoded pixel threshold of y > 700 (approx 64.8% of 1080p) instead of 0.7 (70%)

privileged_info_summary: The task requires generating a bouncing red ball animation using OpenToonz. A synthetic background is generated at '/home/ga/OpenToonz/inputs/room_bg.jpg' with a floor at y=864. The expected output is a PNG sequence of at least 24 frames in '/home/ga/OpenToonz/output/bouncing_ball/'. The verifier extracts frames 1, 12, and 24, expecting a red ball object to be present in all. The ball must drop at least 100 pixels by frame 12, bounce back up by at least 100 pixels by frame 24, and the impact at frame 12 must be below y=700. The verifier uses HSV masking to detect the red ball and checks the presence of the background wall color.
